# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment for our batch processing jobs. The pods run data processing tasks and don't need to interact with the Kubernetes API. Use default service account settings for simplicity. Focus on getting the jobs running reliably.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1  # version of k8s api used in this yml file (apps v1)  
kind: Deployment    # type to create, here it'll be a deployment(batch processing jobs). kind is the name given by Kubernetes for an object. In our case 'Deployment'.
metadata: 
  labels:     # Labels are used as selectors in k8s objects (like pod) and they help us to identify which resources we want to manage or control with a single command, here it'll be identifying the deployment by name "my-job".  
    app: myJob     
  name: myJob     # Name of our object. In this case 'My Job'. It will also serve as an identifier for Kubernetes resources in general terms (like pods or services). Here it'll be named `my_batchprocessing` . The format is lowercase with words separated by hyphens, and the first character must always start a word ('lower-first').
spec: 
  replicas: 1    # Number of Pods that should run in parallel. In this case we want only one pod running at any given time (reliable).  
  selector:      # Selector is used to find the set of Pods which are currently associated with a Deployment, here it'll be identifying our deployment by name "my-job". The format should match 'matchLabels'. It will select pod based on labels. Here `app="MyJob", and tier = batch`
    matchLabels:  # Label keys must always start from the word ('lower case') followed with a hyphen, like this - app : my-job ,tier :batch . The value of these label key/value pairs should be same as in labels.  
      app: "myJob"    
      tier: batch  # Label values are always 'Batch' for the given keys (app and TIER). This ensures that all pods associated with this Deployment will run data processing tasks only when they have these specific label(key/value pairs) set.  
    type: "batch"     # Type of Pod to select, here it should be 'Batch' as we are running batch jobs (data-processing). The format is lowercase and words separated by hyphens in kubernetes terms like this - `type=批处理` .  This ensures that all pods associated with the Deployment will run data processing tasks only when they have these specific label(key/value pairs) set.
    version: "v1"     # Version of Pod to select, here it should be 'V1' as we are running batch jobs (data-processing). The format is lowercase and words separated by hyphens in kubernetes terms like this - `version=批处理` .  This ensures that all pods associated with the Deployment will run data processing tasks only when they have these specific label(key/value pairs) set.
   template:     # Template describes how to create a Pod, here it'll be creating one single instance of our batch job (data-processing). It includes details like image name and version from where the container should start running etc., which are not included in this yml file as they depend on specifics about your application.
     metadata:  # Metadata for Pod, here it'll be setting up labels to identify our pod(batch processing job) with `app="myJob", tier = batch` and version of the image being used is 'image-name/version'.  
       name: myPod      # Name should always start from lower case followed by hyphen, like this - "My Pod". This will be our pod's identifier.  The format must match `matchLabels` in selector section and also it can contain alphanumeric characters with underscores (_) only as per kubernetes naming convention for objects (pod/service).
       labels:          # Label keys should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen, like this - `app="myJob", tier = batch`. This will be used to identify our pod(batch processing job) with these specific label key-value pairs set in selector section and also it can contain alphanumeric characters (like 'image_name/version') only as per kubernetes naming convention for objects like this - `app="myJob", tier = batch, imageName: "batchprocessingImageVersion".
         app: my-job  # Label keys should always start from the word ('lower case'), followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
         tier: batch    # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
         imageName: batchprocessingImageVersion  # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
         version: v1     # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
         resources:       # Resources request & limit should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
           requests:       # Requests should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
             cpu: "100m"     # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
             memory: "128Mi"  # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
           limits:          # Limits should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
             cpu: "500m"     # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
             memory: "256Mi"  # Label values should always start from the word ('lower case') followed by a hyphen and then 'tier' as it is set to be used for identifying our pod(Batch processing job). The value of these label key/value pairs must match `matchLabels` in selector section.
